summary: The packet shows one clear cross-surface tool conflict, one hard internal contradiction, and one repeated method that should move to a shared home. The reviewer also has one readability problem.
next_actions: Remove the tool claim that conflicts with the declared allowed tools in InterviewSummaryWriter
Resolve the five-line limit vs full-analysis conflict in InterviewSummaryWriter
Move the repeated claim-check method into one shared skill or module and point all three targets to it

findings.title: Prose conflicts with declared tool limits
findings.principle_rule: prompt wording must match declared constraints
findings.summary: InterviewSummaryWriter tells the agent to use tools that the declared constraints do not allow.

evidence.label: A
evidence.text: Use any browser or shell tool that seems useful.

related_evidence.label: C
related_evidence.text: Use any browser or shell tool that seems useful.

findings.why_it_matters: The role can ask for tools it is not allowed to use. That creates drift between the prompt and the typed surface that should control it.
findings.recommended_fix: Remove the browser and shell line, or replace it with the exact allowed tools from the constraint surface.
fix_steps: Name only the allowed tools if tool use must stay in the prompt
Keep the constraint surface as the source of truth
findings.suggested_rewrite: Use only the allowed tools: ReadFile and SearchDocs.
findings.good_example: Use only the allowed tools: ReadFile and SearchDocs.
findings.bad_example: Use any browser or shell tool that seems useful.

findings.title: Internal contradiction in answer shape
findings.principle_rule: one role should not demand incompatible outputs
findings.summary: InterviewSummaryWriter asks for both a very short answer and a full exhaustive analysis.

evidence.label: A
evidence.text: Always keep the answer under five lines.

evidence.label: B
evidence.text: Provide a full, exhaustive analysis of every interview theme.

related_evidence.label: C
related_evidence.text:
Always keep the answer under five lines.
Provide a full, exhaustive analysis of every interview theme.

findings.why_it_matters: The agent cannot reliably satisfy both instructions at once. It will either stay short and skip the analysis, or analyze fully and break the length rule.
findings.recommended_fix: Pick one default shape and make the other a conditional exception.
fix_steps: Keep the short output rule if brevity is the real goal
Move the exhaustive analysis into an opt-in exception
Delete the other rule so the role has one clear stop line
findings.suggested_rewrite: Keep the answer brief by default. Expand only when the user asks for depth.
findings.good_example: Keep the answer brief by default. Expand only when the user asks for depth.
findings.bad_example: Always keep the answer under five lines. Provide a full, exhaustive analysis of every interview theme.

findings.title: Repeated rule across agents
findings.principle_rule: one rule should have one owner
findings.summary: The same claim-check method appears in all three targets.

evidence.label: A
evidence.text: Check each claim against a source quote. Mark weak quotes. Remove unsupported claims.

related_evidence.label: B
related_evidence.text:
Check each claim against a source quote.
- Mark weak quotes.
- Remove unsupported claims.

related_evidence.label: C
related_evidence.text:
Check each claim against a source quote.
- Mark weak quotes.
- Remove unsupported claims.

findings.why_it_matters: This rule now has three owners. It will drift as soon as one target changes and the others do not.
findings.recommended_fix: Move the repeated method into one shared skill or module, then point all three targets at it.
fix_steps: Create one shared owner for the quote-check method
Reference that shared owner from all three targets
Delete the local copies from each role
findings.suggested_rewrite: Use the shared quote-check method before you finalize the summary.
findings.good_example: Use the shared quote-check method before you finalize the summary.
findings.bad_example: Check each claim against a source quote. Mark weak quotes. Remove unsupported claims.

findings.title: Reading level too high
findings.principle_rule: keep role text direct and easy to read
findings.summary: InterviewSummaryReviewer uses wording that is much harder to read than the rest of the packet.

evidence.label: A
evidence.text: Downstream operators should independently operationalize the artifact-level implications of the present intervention.

related_evidence.label: B
related_evidence.text: Downstream operators should independently operationalize the artifact-level implications of the present intervention.

related_evidence.label: C
related_evidence.text: Downstream operators should independently operationalize the artifact-level implications of the present intervention.

findings.why_it_matters: Dense abstract wording slows down readers and makes the handoff harder to use.
findings.recommended_fix: Say the same thing in plain words.
fix_steps: Replace the abstract handoff line with direct language
Name the artifact or note the reviewer should leave behind
findings.suggested_rewrite: Tell the next role what changed and what note to keep.
findings.good_example: Tell the next role what changed and what note to keep.
findings.bad_example: Downstream operators should independently operationalize the artifact-level implications of the present intervention.
